text.title: LB Layer Selection Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed in LB layer decisions. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Architecture doc says "L4 for performance" then mentions URL-path routing + header rewrite. Implementation team builds a port-per-path NLB hack that nobody can maintain.
antipattern.root-cause: Author equated "L4" with "fast" without checking whether the proposed features actually exist at L4.
antipattern.fix: Replace L4 with L7 (accept the CPU cost) OR split into hybrid L4-front-of-L7. Reject inconsistency at review.

antipattern.symptom: Database proxy fronted by ALB; throughput tops out at 30 % of expected; HTTP parsing CPU dominates while the actual traffic is raw Postgres.
antipattern.root-cause: Author defaulted to "L7 for everything" without checking the protocol.
antipattern.fix: Switch to L4 (NLB / HAProxy `mode tcp`). Throughput jumps; CPU drops by an order of magnitude.

antipattern.symptom: Architecture promises "sub-second failover" via Route 53 with TTL = 30 s; SLO measurement shows 5-minute failover for some users.
antipattern.root-cause: L3/DNS-based GSLB selected at the layer-decision step even though SLO requires anycast (a Layer-3 BGP construct).
antipattern.fix: Move global LB to anycast (AWS Global Accelerator / GCP Global LB / Cloudflare LB) and treat the regional ALB / Ingress as the L7 tier behind it.
